rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Appointment Of Elisor To Execute Documents On Behalf Of Prestina Trias Macias; Declaration Of Counsel In Support Of Motion For Appointment Of Elisor
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Co-Conservators Lauro Trias and Ramona Figueira's Motion for Appointment of Elisor to Execute Documents. No opposition or response to the Motion was filed with the court. As often happens in probate proceedings, several of the parties to this matter share a surname. To reduce confusion, the court refers to parties by their first names in this tentative ruling, with no disrespect intended. The following is a summary of the factual and procedural history leading to the instant Motion for Appointment of Elisor to Execute Documents: In August 2004, Leonisa Trias executed a trust, but did not fund it. In 2015, she executed a Durable Power of Attorney, nominating her son Lauro Trias as her agent. From 2016 to 2019, her care and finances were managed by her children Lauro, Ramona Figueria, and Prestina Macias. Prestina lived with her mother and was her primary caregiver. In or around early 2019, Leonisa executed a second Durable Power of Attorney, nominating Prestina as her agent. A grant deed dated April 17, 2019 transferred ownership of 121 Seneca Ave., San Francisco, California out of Leonisa's name and into Prestina and her husband Carlos' ownership. On September 4, 2019, Lauro and Ramona filed Petition for Conservator of Person and Estate of Leonisa Trias. The petition was granted on July 6, 2020, over opposition by Leonisa and Prestina. On July 6, 2020, the court ordered the matter to pro bono mediation to address the parties' disputes over Leonisa's medical care, Prestina allegedly isolating Leonisa from her family and nominated agents, and the contested real property transfer of 121 Seneca Avenue and related financial activity by Prestina and Carlos. These mediation efforts and further court direction eventually yielded on Amended Settlement Agreement. The parties to the Settlement were four of Leonisa's seven children: Lauro Trias, Ramona Figueria, Lazaro Trias, Jr., Prestina Macias, and Prestina's husband, Carlos C. Macias. Relevant to this Motion, the terms of the settlement addressed the following: the Probate Code section 850 and Disinheritance Petition the Co-Conservators had filed in the conservatorship; transferring title to 121 Seneca Avenue out of Prestina and Carlos' names and back to the Conservatee's name, with plans to ultimately transfer title to the 2004 trust; liens and other potential claims Prestina and Carlos had against the conservatorship estate; Prestina, Carlos, and Lazaro's continued residence at 121 Seneca Avenue during conservatee's lifetime; and, sale of 121 Seneca Avenue after Conservatee's death. On November 12, 2021, this court entered Order Granting Petition for Approval of Amended Settlement Agreement, and retained jurisdiction under Code of Civil Proc. section 664.6, per the terms of the Settlement. On November 19, 2021, pursuant to the Settlement, Co-Conservators dismissed their Probate Code section 850 and Disinheritance petition. On January 13, 2022, the court granted Co-Conservator's Ex Parte Application for Order Voiding of Prior Transfer Document, pursuant to the Amended Settlement Agreement. The order voided the document executed on April 17, 2019 transferring title to the real property located at 121 Seneca Avenue from Leonisa to Prestina and Carlos, and ruled the property reverted back to Leonisa as her separate property. Leonisa died on January 6, 2022, intestate. Despite the stated intention of the 2021 Amended Settlement Agreement the real property had not been transferred back to her exclusive ownership by the time of her death. On October 25, 2022, Lauro was appointed Administrator of the probate estate with limited authority under IAEA and $20,000 bond, in related case number PES-22-305443.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Appointment Of Elisor To Execute Documents On Behalf Of Prestina Trias Macias; Declaration Of Counsel In Support Of Motion For Appointment Of Elisor (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] On October 20, 2023, the court entered Order Granting Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement. Co-conservators served Notice of Entry of Order on November 2, 2023. Here, the instant Motion is brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128(a)(4). Under this provision, "Every court shall have the power to do all of the following:[] To compel obedience in its judgments, orders, and process, and to the orders of a judge out of court, in an action or proceeding therein." Petitioners cite Blueberry Properties, LLC, v. Chow (2014) 230 Cal. App. 4th 1017 as supporting authority, which the court finds persuasive on this record. Per the Motion, all parties have performed their portion of the Settlement Agreement except for Prestina, who allegedly continues to refuse to perform pursuant to the Amended Settlement Agreement or in compliance with the court's October 20, 2023 Order. Specifically, she has refused to execute the necessary documents to undo the Grant Deed dated April 17, 2019 transferring ownership in 121 Seneca Ave, San Francisco, CA from Leonisa to Prestina and Carlos to transfer ownership of the property back to her late mother's estate. On the basis of Prestina's failure to comply with the October 20, 2023 Order, Co-Conservators seek an order of this court appointing an elisor to execute all necessary documents to transfer title to the real property located at 121 Seneca Avenue, San Francisco, CA, back to the estate of Leonisa Trias. An elisor is a person appointed by the court to perform functions like the execution of a deed or document. Blueberry Properties, LLC v. Chow (2014), 230 Cal. App. 4th 1017, 1020; see also, Rayan v. Dykeman (1990) 224 Cal. App. 3d 1629, 1635 fn. 2. The Motion further seeks leave of court to request any further appointment of an elisor in this matter on an ex parte basis, without notice or appearance by the parties. The unopposed Motion for Appointment of Elisor is GRANTED, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128(a)(4), and the court's inherent power to compel obedience to it orders. Consistent with this tentative ruling, Co-Conservators shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 the quit claim deed submitted as Exhibit B to the Motion, with all other documents necessary to transfer title to the real property located at 121 Seneca Avenue, San Francisco, CA, to the estate of Leonisa Trias. The Clerk of Court is thereafter ordered to execute on behalf of Prestina Trias aka Prestina Macias, and pursuant to the October 20, 2023 Order Granting Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement, the submitted quit claim deed and all other documents necessary to transfer title to the real property located at 121 Seneca Avenue, San Francisco, CA, to the estate of Leonisa Trias, that have been lodged with the clerk in Department 204 in compliance with this Order. The moving party's request for leave to seek further court-appointment of an elisor in this matter without notice or appearance is DENIED.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Appointment Of Elisor To Execute Documents On Behalf Of Prestina Trias Macias; Declaration Of Counsel In Support Of Motion For Appointment Of Elisor (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling).
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Co-Conservators shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
